title: chrysosplenium pilosum subsp. schagae
intro: Chrysosplenium pilosum subsp. schagae, also known as Schaga's wood sorrel, is a rare subspecies of flowering plant in the wood sorrel family, Saxifragaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and possessing antimicrobial properties. Traditionally, Chrysosplenium pilosum subsp. schagae has been used in herbal preparations, such as infusions and tinctures, to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
